optionsDescription: Set the key, subject, and SAN entries that go into the certificate signing request.
importDescription: Paste an unencrypted PKCS#8 PEM private key. Encrypted, PKCS#1, or SEC1 keys are not supported.
subjectDescription: All fields are optional, but most CAs expect at least a Common Name.
sanDescription: One entry per line or comma separated. Leave a section blank to omit it.
outputDescription: Copy or download the certificate signing request, and the generated private key if a new one was created.
emptyDescription: Fill in the form and select Generate CSR to create a request.
errorMissingSubjectOrSan: Provide at least one Subject field or SAN entry.
errorMissingPrivateKey: Paste a PKCS#8 PEM private key before generating.
errorInvalidPem: The private key input is not a valid PEM block.
errorUnsupportedPem: Unsupported PEM block. Paste a PRIVATE KEY block.
errorLegacyPem: Legacy PEM format. Convert to PKCS#8 with: openssl pkcs8 -topk8 -nocrypt -in legacy.pem -out pkcs8.pem
errorEncryptedKey: Encrypted private keys are not supported. Decrypt with openssl pkcs8 first.
errorUnsupportedKeyType: Only RSA and ECDSA private keys are supported.
errorUnsupportedCurve: Only the P-256, P-384, and P-521 curves are supported.
errorWebCryptoUnavailable: This browser does not expose the Web Crypto APIs required to sign the CSR.
errorImportFailed: The private key could not be imported. Confirm it is a valid PKCS#8 PEM and try again.
errorGenerationFailed: CSR generation failed. Try a different algorithm or refresh the page.
csrDescription: Submit this PEM block to the certificate authority that will sign it.
privateKeyDescription: The matching unencrypted PKCS#8 private key. Store it securely before requesting a certificate.
privateKeyWarningDescription: Nothing leaves your browser. The generated private key is unencrypted, so move it into a secure key store before using it.
